candidates.notes: Probe Quality Gate result: GO_WITH_CAUTION. Mean CV F1=0.773, median=0.893, across 29 countries with 189 single-token cities. 16/29 countries achieve F1>0.85, 18/29 achieve F1>0.80, 22/29 above 0.70. Three countries (Argentina, Egypt, Greece) show F1=0.0 due to very small samples (3-4 cities each). Notably, SAE probes OUTPERFORM dense probes (0.773 vs 0.617 mean F1), indicating that the SAE captures geographic knowledge well. Strong performers (F1=1.0): Australia, Canada, China, India, Indonesia, Italy, Japan, Mexico, Netherlands, South Africa, Thailand, Turkey. Weak performers: Argentina (0.0, n=4), Brazil (0.47, n=6), Egypt (0.0, n=3), Greece (0.0, n=3), Portugal (0.4, n=5). The weak performers are predominantly countries with few (3-5) cities. Decision: PROCEED with caution. For full cross-domain experiments, exclude countries with <5 cities or F1<0.50, and report probe quality per-country as a key caveat.

first_letter_validation.condition: absorption rate differs >3x from published 15-35% range
first_letter_validation.action: PROCEED with refinement - rate is within range but controls need calibration

probe_quality_pilot.condition: F1 < 0.70 -> pivot; 0.70 <= F1 < 0.85 -> caution; F1 >= 0.85 -> proceed
probe_quality_pilot.action: Proceed with caution; report probe quality as limitation. Countries with F1<0.50 should be excluded from absorption measurement or flagged.

refinement_plan: For full cross-domain experiments: exclude countries with <5 cities or probe F1<0.50
Report per-country probe F1 prominently alongside absorption rates
Increase per-letter word count from 25 to 50+ for better first-letter probe quality
Calibrate cosine threshold using null distribution for first-letter absorption
Consider stratifying absorption analysis by probe quality tier (F1>0.85 vs 0.70-0.85)
City-continent and city-language probes may perform better due to fewer classes
